channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from global sources

item.title: US strikes Iranian tankers as Gulf tensions threaten ceasefire
item.description: US forces struck tankers attempting to break Iran blockade in the Gulf of Oman; Iran questioned American diplomacy seriousness amid renewed clashes.

item.title: China unveils worlds first dual-core quantum computer
item.description: Chinas Hanyuan-2 quantum computer operates at room temperature, marking a breakthrough in quantum computing accessibility and maintenance.

item.title: Japans Mogami-class warship gaining ground in New Zealand defense competition
item.description: Japans Mogami frigate and Britains Type 31 are top contenders for New Zealands naval modernization, with Australia potentially influencing the outcome.

item.title: Ukraine deploys AI-powered turrets against fiber-optic drones
item.description: Ukrainian forces successfully deployed autonomous AI-driven turrets capable of intercepting Russian drones immune to electronic warfare.

item.title: Bahrain arrests 41 people linked to Irans IRGC
item.description: Bahrains interior ministry announced the arrest of 41 individuals with alleged ties to Irans Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

item.title: Putin denounces NATO at scaled-back Victory Day parade
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin used his annual Victory Day speech to justify the war in Ukraine and criticize NATO, while no armored vehicles or ballistic missiles featured in Moscow parade for the first time in years. North Korean soldiers participated alongside Russian troops.

item.title: Iran accuses US of reckless military adventure amid Hormuz clashes
item.description: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi accused the US of opting for military action over diplomacy as US forces disabled two Iranian tankers and fired on more Iranian vessels. Qatari PM urges negotiations as ceasefire holds despite ongoing exchanges.

item.title: Hungary prepares for regime change as Magyar set to become PM
item.description: Péter Magyar is being sworn in as Hungary new prime minister after his Tisza party won a landslide victory, ending 16 years of Viktor Orbán rule. The new government inherits a grim economic situation and promises asset recovery investigations.

item.title: Hantavirus cruise ship heads to Tenerife as nations track passengers
item.description: A cruise ship with confirmed hantavirus cases is approaching Tenerife as health authorities across four continents track dozens of passengers who disembarked before the outbreak was detected. Three people have died and five infections confirmed.

item.title: Virginia Supreme Court strikes down Democratic redistricting plan
item.description: Virginia highest court ruled 4-3 that Democrats violated procedural requirements when placing the congressional redistricting amendment on the ballot, delivering a setback to the party in the nationwide battle for House control ahead of midterms.

item.title: Bahrain Arrests 41 People Linked to Iran's IRGC
item.description: Bahrain's interior ministry announced the arrest of 41 individuals it says were connected to Iran's Revolutionary Guards, amid heightened regional tensions following the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: Trump Announces Russia-Ukraine 3-Day Ceasefire Agreement
item.description: President Trump said both Putin and Zelenskyy agreed to a three-day ceasefire and prisoner exchange, calling it a potential "beginning of the end" of the war.

item.title: Venice Biennale Torn by Controversy Over Russian Participation
item.description: Russia's return to the Venice Biennale has sparked outrage, with Ukraine's culture minister comparing it to "inviting a serial killer to dinner with your friends" as the art world grapples with war's cultural impact.

item.title: Israeli artillery shells southern Lebanon, destroys homes in new strikes
item.description: Israeli forces continue targeting towns in southern Lebanon despite ceasefire arrangements, with artillery strikes hitting Braashit, Safad al-Battikh, and Touline amid ongoing truce violations.

item.title: Erdogan says Europe without Türkiye is incomplete and vulnerable in crisis management
item.description: Marking Europe Day, President Erdogan emphasized Turkiye as an essential partner for European security and crisis response, calling for a more inclusive EU amid global challenges.

item.title: France seeks Africa reset at Kenya summit after West Africa setbacks
item.description: Paris aims to rebuild influence in Africa following losses in former colonies, co-hosting a regional summit in Kenya as part of a strategic pivot away from its troubled West African relationships.

item.title: Ukraine running critically low on air defence missiles after winter campaign
item.description: Ukraine's air force warned it is running short of air defence missiles following Russia's massive winter attack campaign, as Kyiv braces for further strikes amid ongoing conflict.

item.title: Zelenskiy confirms US-brokered three-day ceasefire and prisoner exchange with Russia
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy confirmed a three-day ceasefire arranged with Russia as part of US efforts to negotiate an end to the over four-year-old war.

item.title: Virginia Supreme Court throws out Democratic congressional map, boosts Republican midterm hopes
item.description: The court rejected a Democratic-backed ballot measure that reconfigured the state's US House districts for partisan advantage, dealing a major blow to Democratic midterm prospects.

item.title: US trade chief says Trump administration will win appeal on temporary tariffs ruling
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said the administration expects to prevail in appealing the Court of International Trade's ruling against its temporary 10% global tariffs.

item.title: US and Iran Remain Deadlocked Despite Ongoing Gulf Clashes
item.description: Washington awaits Tehran's response to a US proposal to formally end the war, but neither side appears closer to resolution as military confrontations continue in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: US Warns EU of Higher Tariffs if Trade Deal Misses July 4 Deadline
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer stated that the US will revert to higher tariffs on European goods if Brussels fails to implement trade commitments by the July 4 deadline.

item.title: India appoints new Chief of Defence Staff amid major military reforms
item.description: Retired Lt. Gen. NS Raja Subramani named India's next Chief of Defence Staff, succeeding Anil Chauhan as the country undertakes its biggest military overhaul to modernize and integrate the army, navy, and air force.

item.title: Plaid Cymru wins most seats in Wales Senedd election, ready to form government
item.description: Rhun ap Iorwerth's Plaid Cymru won the most seats in the Welsh Parliament election, signalling a shift in Welsh politics as the party prepares to form a new government after a historic victory.

item.title: Russian casualties in Ukraine reach 1.34 million, says Kyiv
item.description: Ukraine's General Staff reports Russia has lost approximately 1,340,270 troops since the full-scale invasion began in February 2022, in one of the highest casualty tolls of any modern conflict.
